biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:stable — units -4 (rg via Bash for symbol discovery, ls via Bash for directory listing), -6 (rg via Bash for section anchoring before every Edit block), -8 (rg via Bash for section anchoring on 6 of 17 tool calls), -21 (rg -l and rg -n via Bash for file and pattern discovery), -32 (/usr/bin/grep explicit path used 7 times for symbol lookup, section anchoring, pagination prop scanning, TeamFilter usage scan), -34 (/usr/bin/grep -c and -n plus cat -n via Bash instead of Read); 6 of 10 non-trivial units; frequency, structure, and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form identical to all 4 prior cycles; zero internalization

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:stable — unit -6: Read then 8 Edit calls on plan.md with 4 interleaved rg anchors and 3 interleaved Reads; unit -8: failed Edit then rg anchor then 6 Edit calls on plan.md with 5 interleaved rg anchors and 4 Reads; unit -32: 3 Edit calls on data-history-v3.tsx (2 with interleaved Reads mid-cycle) plus 6 Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx; peak count stable at 6-8 Edits per file, identical structure to prior 4 cycles

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:confirmed — unit -8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg anchor + Read + successful Edit; unit -34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before grep anchor + successful Edit; was flagged RECURRING in prior cycle, still present with same two-unit frequency per session

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:stable — unit -8 opens with blind Edit on plan.md that fails, requiring grep probe to locate content; unit -34 opens with blind Edit on file-select.v2.tsx that fails; unit -32: find+grep chain for ModalId before confirming file path; consistent with prior flagging
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations has appeared in every cycle with zero improvement trend — prior cycle recommendations have not been internalized; escalation path (hook or CLAUDE.md inline rule requiring Grep before Bash for file content search) has not been enacted; this pattern is a candidate for hook-based enforcement rather than recommendation

re_edit_thrash has not improved across 5 cycles; the rg-anchor-before-Edit workflow is being used as a substitute for holding file state in context; compaction or long-session context loss may be the root cause — checkpoint more aggressively before multi-Edit sequences on large planning documents

optimistic_edit_without_read is confirmed recurring; a PreToolUse hook that checks whether a Read has occurred on the target file within the last N tool calls would catch this mechanically without relying on agent internalization
